response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings genuine breadth — 15+ years of full-stack and open-source development, Linux exposure, and architecture work across diverse stacks — but falls short on several non-negotiable criteria for this role. The most disqualifying gap is German proficiency: the role requires a minimum C1, and the resume explicitly lists German as 'Basic,' which would immediately block client-facing advisory and domestic collaboration. Additionally, there is no evidence of formal team leadership or mentoring at scale, no Python in the skill set despite it being a desired technical skill, and no mention of UML or structured requirements management — all of which are central to the Head of Development mandate.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on software development spanning web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems, demonstrating genuine technical versatility
Founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform — direct evidence of open-source commitment and platform architecture ownership
Public knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, FOSS4G talk, open-source libraries) aligns with the consulting and advisory dimension of the role
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — a hard blocker against the C1 minimum required for client advisory and domestic operations
No documented team leadership, headcount management, or mentoring-at-scale experience; career history is predominantly individual contributor roles
Python absent from the skill set and UML/requirements management not mentioned, despite both being explicitly listed as desired or required competencies
